title: Contributing to ofemeantest
text: Thanks interest improving package. document covers local dev setup expect contributors opening PR.

title: Contributing to ofemeantest
text: Clone open project R IDE: Install dev dependencies (one-): Enable project’s git hooks (one-per clone). points git versioned .githooks/ directory pre-push check runs every git push: can verify took effect git config --get core.hooksPath (print .githooks).
code: git clone https://github.com/PPaccioretti/ofemeantest.git cd ofemeantest install.packages(c("devtools", "roxygen2", "testthat", "pkgdown")) git config core.hooksPath .githooks

title: Contributing to ofemeantest
text: every git push hook runs devtools::document() checks whether anything man/, NAMESPACE DESCRIPTION changed. , push blocked list files commit. prevents common source “stale docs” PRs — editing roxygen comment R/foo.R forgetting regenerate .Rd. bypass hook single push (e.g., emergency hotfix doc regen unrelated): check runs GitHub Actions every PR (.github/workflows/document.yaml), even pushes bypass local hook caught review time.
code: git push --no-verify

title: Contributing to ofemeantest
text: docs/ directory gitignored — ’s used local preview rebuilt CI push main.
code: devtools::test()       # run testthat suite devtools::check()      # full R CMD check pkgdown::build_site()  # rebuild the docs site under docs/

title: Contributing to ofemeantest
text: Keep functions documented roxygen2; mark internals @keywords internal. Add tests new public behaviour. Contract tests tests/testthat/test-dependency-contracts.R guard interfaces rely spdep, spatialreg, permuco multcompView — add new entry start depending new upstream function. Prefer base R dplyr/tidyr keep Imports list small.

title: Getting started with ofemeantest
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("PPaccioretti/ofemeantest")

title: Getting started with ofemeantest
text: bundled ofe_f2 dataset comes corn (Zea mays L.) trial single fertilized strip (~2.2 ha) compared adjacent control strip field. Raw yield-monitor data cleaned following Vega et al. (2019).

title: Getting started with ofemeantest
text: Call ofemt() let build grid internally: Key fields printed result: Selected cells: cells retained filtering single treatment min_per_cell. n / ESS: nominal sample size effective sample size given spatial autocorrelation residuals. Rho / Moran’s : spatial autocorrelation indicators. Means comparison: per-treatment median yield (cells) compact letter display multiple-comparison procedure. ANOVA permutation test: median p-value per pairwise comparison.
code: res <- ofemt(   data = ofe_f2,   y = "Yield_tn",   x = "Treatment",   cellsize = 9,   min_per_cell = 4,   n_p = 2000,   n_s = 200,   alpha = 0.05 ) res #>  #> === OFE permutation analysis === #> Cellsize: 9_9 | Selected cells: 554 #> Obs/cell (min/median/max): 4 / 5 / 7 #> n: 554 | ESS: 97 | Rho: 0.652 | Moran's I: 0.548 #>  #> --- Means comparison --- #>   Treatment Yield_tn_mean letters #>  Fertilized      5.280402       b #>     Control      4.760095      a  #>  #> --- Pairwise tests (median p-value across runs, corrected p-value) --- #>              Comparison p_value  p_adj #>  Control vs. Fertilized  0.0035 0.0035

title: Getting started with ofemeantest
text: finer control — inspecting grid, tweaking cell size, reusing selection several downstream analyses — build grid explicitly make_ofe_grid() pass ofemt().  calls produce numeric output grid built matching arguments — ofemt(grid = NULL, ...) equivalent ofemt(grid = make_ofe_grid(...)) hood.
code: g <- make_ofe_grid(   data = ofe_f2,   x = "Treatment",   cellsize = 9,   min_per_cell = 4 ) names(g) #> [1] "grid_all"   "grid_sel"   "params"     "cell_stats"   plot_grid_selection(g) res2 <- ofemt(   data = ofe_f2,   y = "Yield_tn",   x = "Treatment",   grid = g,   n_p = 2000,   n_s = 200 )

title: Getting started with ofemeantest
text: ofemt() exposes seed argument controls random subsampling inside permutation runs. default seed = 7L, two calls inputs return identical p-values: Pass seed = NULL let results vary across runs (e.g., exploring sensitivity random draws).
code: identical(   ofemt(     ofe_f2,     y = "Yield_tn",     x = "Treatment",     cellsize = 9,     min_per_cell = 4,     seed = 7L   ),   ofemt(     ofe_f2,     y = "Yield_tn",     x = "Treatment",     cellsize = 9,     min_per_cell = 4,     seed = 7L   ) ) #> TRUE

title: Getting started with ofemeantest
text: call retains per-run p-values res$perm_runs can sanity-check median p-value artefact long tail:
code: # Requires the optional 'ggplot2' package. plot_pvalue_hist(res)

title: Getting started with ofemeantest
text: vignette("methodology") — technical description protocol (effective sample size, permutational ANOVA, multiplicity adjustment). ?ofemt — full argument reference main function. ?make_ofe_grid — details grid construction selection.

title: Getting started with ofemeantest
text: Córdoba M., Paccioretti P., Balzarini M. (review). new method compare treatments unreplicated -farm experimentation. Griffith, D.. (2005). Effective geographic sample size presence spatial autocorrelation. Annals Association American Geographers 95(4): 740–760. Vega ., Córdoba M., Balzarini M. (2019). Protocol automating error removal yield maps. Precision Agriculture 20: 1030–1044. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11119-018-09632-8

title: Methodology
text: -farm experiments (OFE) commercial fields typically laid side--side strips replication treatments randomisation. Yield-monitor data strips dense, irregular strongly spatially autocorrelated, classical ANOVA raw observations grossly inflates effective sample size breaks independence assumption. protocol implemented adapts ANOVA setting () aggregating raw observations regular grid, (ii) estimating autocorrelation-corrected effective sample size, (iii) inferring treatment effects distribution permutational p-values computed random subsamples ESS.

title: Methodology
text: make_ofe_grid() builds regular grid (square cells, optional rotation origin shift) bounding box trial data assigns yield-monitor point cell. cell selected analysis : contains least min_per_cell observations, observations come single treatment. Mixed-treatment cells (straddle boundary strips) dropped. per-cell response used downstream median raw observations falling cell, robust typical outliers yield-monitor data. Implementation details: grid origin cell size passed deterministically; arguments always produce cells, ofemt(grid = NULL, ...) ofemt(grid = make_ofe_grid(...)) interchangeable.

title: Methodology
text: one-way ANOVA cell medians yields residuals \(e_i\). spatial autocorrelation estimated approximate profile likelihood estimator (APLE) \(\rho\) (Li, Calder & Cressie, 2007), effective sample size computed Griffith (2005): \[ n^{*} = n \left(1 - \cdot \frac{n-1}{n} \cdot \left(1 - \exp(-b\rho + c\sqrt{\rho})\right)\right) \] coefficients given published formula. also report Moran’s residuals sanity check magnitude autocorrelation. \(\rho\) constrained \([0, 1]\) entering ESS formula.

title: Methodology
text: Given ESS, ofemt() runs n_s independent permutational ANOVAs. run: samples \(\lceil n^{*}/k \rceil\) cells without replacement \(k\) treatments; fits one-way ANOVA subsample; permutes treatment labels n_p times records proportion permuted F-statistics exceed observed one (per-run p-value). reported test statistic comparison median n_s per-run p-values. full distribution retained res$perm_runs can inspected plot_pvalue_hist().

title: Methodology
text: trial contains two treatments, every pairwise comparison tested resulting median p-values adjusted multiplicity via stats::p.adjust() (Bonferroni, Holm Benjamini–Hochberg, selected p_adjust_method argument). compact letter display computed adjusted p-values multcompView::multcompLetters().

title: Methodology
text: protocol assumes trial layout produces enough cells per treatment subsampling step meaningful. ESS formula approximation valid approximately normally distributed data non-negative autocorrelation. current implementation supports single categorical treatment. Multi-factor designs, factor interactions continuous covariates yet supported.

title: Methodology
text: Córdoba M., Paccioretti P., Balzarini M. (review). new method compare treatments unreplicated -farm experimentation. Griffith, D.. (2005). Effective geographic sample size presence spatial autocorrelation. Annals Association American Geographers 95(4): 740–760. Li, H., Calder, C.. & Cressie, N. (2007). Beyond Moran’s : Testing spatial dependence based spatial autoregressive model. Geographical Analysis 39(4): 357–375. Vega ., Córdoba M., Balzarini M. (2019). Protocol automating error removal yield maps. Precision Agriculture 20: 1030–1044.

code: @Manual{,   title = {ofemeantest: On Farm Experimentation Mean Test},   author = {Mariano Córdoba and Pablo Paccioretti and Monica Balzarini},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.900-1},   url = {https://ppaccioretti.github.io/ofemeantest/}, }

title: ofemeantest — On-Farm Experimentation Mean Test
text: can install development version ofemeantest GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("PPaccioretti/ofemeantest")

title: ofemeantest — On-Farm Experimentation Mean Test
text: example dataset comprised field trial corn (Zea mays L.), impact broadcast phosphorus fertilization evaluated. treatment strip received superphosphate rates ranging 250 500 kgha–1kg\ ha^{–1} [0-21-0], control strip received P fertilizer (0 kgha–1kg\ ha^{–1}). strip 2.2 ha area, totaling 100 ha. Raw grain yield monitor data single strip cleaned following protocol proposed Vega et al. (2019). ofemt function first creates regular artificial grid area, including strips analyzed. grid spacing (cellsize) set 9 m. grid overlaid point data yield map, assigning georeferenced data point corresponding grid cell. grid cell contained minimum four data points (nmin_cell = 4). Empty cells cells less five data points removed. median yield data calculated cell. one-way ANOVA fitted cell medians compare treatments. residuals fitted model, magnitude spatial autocorrelation estimated using approximate profile likelihood estimator spatial dependence (Rho) Moran index (MI). effective sample size (ESS) calculated. Second, ofemt randomly samples grid cells without replacement using ESS. yield data strips (treatment control) used perform permutational ANOVA 2000 iterations per test (n_p). procedure provides p-value comparison treatment means. permutational ANOVA repeated 200 random samples grid cells (n_s) using sampling procedure. significance level comparing means (alpha), left default value (0.05). resulting permutation p-values collected generate empirical p-value distribution. Finally, median p-value calculated compare means. results show total number cells selected (554), ESS 97, magnitude autocorrelation (Rho) 0.65. fertilization treatment showed significant differences compared control (p < 0.05). average yield fertilized strip 5.28 tha−1t\ ha^{-1}, control 4.76 tha−1t\ ha^{-1}.
code: library(ofemeantest) data("ofe_f2") ofemt(data = ofe_f2,       y = "Yield_tn",       x = "Treatment",       cellsize = 9,       nmin_cell = 4,       n_p = 2000,       n_s = 200,       alpha = 0.05,       shift = 0,       alpha_bonferroni = FALSE,       crs = NULL) #> $`General information` #>   Cellsize Min.Obs.Cell Max.Obs.Cell Median.Obs.Cell   n ESS      Rho        MI #> 1        9            4            7               5 554  97 0.652285 0.5483085 #>  #> $`Cells per treatment` #>  #>    Control Fertilized  #>        273        281  #>  #> --- Pairwise tests (median p-value across runs, corrected p-value) --- #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   Comparison             p_value  p_adj #>   <chr>                    <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 Control vs. Fertilized  0.0208 0.0208

title: ofemeantest — On-Farm Experimentation Mean Test

what: Build OFE grid and (initially) select all cells — all_cells_grid
title: Build OFE grid and (initially) select all cells — all_cells_grid
text: Build OFE grid (initially) select cells

title: Build OFE grid and (initially) select all cells — all_cells_grid
code: all_cells_grid(data, cellsize, angle_deg = 0, buffer = 0, shift = c(0, 0))

title: Build OFE grid and (initially) select all cells — all_cells_grid
text: data `sf` object points geometry. cellsize numeric length-1 length-2, grid cell size. angle_deg numeric, grid rotation degrees. buffer numeric, buffer added around data bbox gridding (units CRS). shift numeric length-2, offset added grid origin (xmin, ymin).

title: Build OFE grid and (initially) select all cells — all_cells_grid
text: object class `ofe_grid` (list) : `grid_all`: `sf` polygons full grid (`CellID`). `grid_sel`: initially identical `grid_all` (filtering yet). `params`: named list grid parameters (cellsize, shift, angle, buffer, CRS).

what: Filter OFE grid cells containing a single treatment — filter_per_treatment
title: Filter OFE grid cells containing a single treatment — filter_per_treatment
text: Performs spatial join point data (`sf`) OFE grid, identifies cells containing exactly one unique treatment, returns updated `ofe_grid` object eligible cells retained `grid_sel` component.

title: Filter OFE grid cells containing a single treatment — filter_per_treatment
code: filter_per_treatment(data, grid, x)

title: Filter OFE grid cells containing a single treatment — filter_per_treatment
text: data `sf` object points containing treatment column `x`. grid `ofe_grid` object (returned [make_grid()] similar). `grid_sel` component used replaced filtered version. Must polygonal share CRS `data`. x character string length one giving name treatment column `data`.

title: Filter OFE grid cells containing a single treatment — filter_per_treatment
text: updated `ofe_grid` object whose `grid_sel` component contains   polygons corresponding single-treatment cells.

title: Filter OFE grid cells containing a single treatment — filter_per_treatment
text: function: Validates input types column name. Replaces spaces treatment labels dots stores         temporary column `".trt"`. Ensures grid `"CellID"` column (creates one missing). Joins points grid cells via `sf::st_intersects()`. Aggregates per cell keeps cells containing single unique         treatment (`unique_trt == 1`). Points outside grid dropped (`left = FALSE` join).

title: Filter OFE grid cells containing a single treatment — filter_per_treatment
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   filtered_grid <- filter_per_treatment(data = pts_sf, grid = my_ofe_grid, x = "Treatment")   plot(filtered_grid$grid_sel["CellID"]) } # }

title: Create an OFE grid and select valid cells — make_ofe_grid

See [all_cells_grid()]. min_per_cell Minimum observations per cell. See [select_grid()]. angle_deg Grid rotation degrees. See [all_cells_grid()]. buffer Buffer applied data bounding box gridding. See [all_cells_grid()]. shift Offset applied grid origin. See [all_cells_grid()]. return_points Logical. See [select_grid()].

title: Create an OFE grid and select valid cells — make_ofe_grid

title: OFE permutation analysis — ofemt
text: Runs cell-based OFE analysis permutation ANOVA spatial diagnostics.

title: OFE permutation analysis — ofemt
code: ofemt(   data,   y,   x,   cellsize = 10,   min_per_cell = 4,   nmin_cell = NULL,   n_p = 1000,   n_s = 200,   alpha = 0.05,   shift = c(0, 0),   p_adjust_method = c("none", "bonferroni", "holm", "BH"),   crs = NULL,   keep_components = c("none", "light", "full"),   grid = NULL,   angle_deg = 0,   buffer = 0,   seed = 7L,   alpha_bonferroni = NULL )

title: OFE permutation analysis — ofemt
text: data sf points; must contain columns `y` `x`. y response column (numeric). x treatment column (factor/character). cellsize, shift, angle_deg, buffer, min_per_cell grid settings (used `grid` NULL). nmin_cell, alpha_bonferroni **Deprecated.** Use `min_per_cell` instead `nmin_cell`, `p_adjust_method = "bonferroni"` instead `alpha_bonferroni`. n_p number permutations per ANOVA run. n_s number sampling runs. alpha significance threshold letters. p_adjust_method p-value adjustment method across pairwise comparisons. crs optional target projected CRS `data` lon/lat. keep_components `"none"`, `"light"`, `"full"` embed geometries output. grid optional: `ofe_grid` (e.g., [make_ofe_grid()]). `NULL`, grid generated internally using `cellsize`, `min_per_cell`, `shift`, `angle_deg` `buffer`, informative message emitted. seed integer; controls reproducibility permutation sampling (grid deterministic construction arguments). Set `seed = NULL` let results vary across runs.

title: OFE permutation analysis — ofemt
text: object class `ofemt_result`. list length 4 mean test comparisson results

title: OFE permutation analysis — ofemt
text: methodology involves: calculation effective sample size (ESS) given underlying spatial structure. ANOVA permutation test random sample ESS. generation empirical distribution p-values repetition step two. median empirical distribution regarded p-value associated non-treatment effect hypothesis. test can easily extended cover scenarios two treatments employing pairwise comparisons OFE across multiple treatments, p-values can adjusted multiplicity using Bonferroni correction

title: OFE permutation analysis — ofemt
text: new method compare treatments unreplicated -farm experimentation. Córdoba M., Paccioretti P., Balzarini M. review.

title: OFE permutation analysis — ofemt
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   my_data <- ofe_f2   res <- ofemt(my_data, y = "Yield", x = "Treatment",                cellsize = 10, min_per_cell = 4, alpha = 0.05) } # }

text: `ggplot` object **ggplot2** installed; otherwise   function falls back base R `hist()` returns `NULL` invisibly.

title: Print method for `ofemt_result` objects — print.ofemt_result
text: Displays concise summary [`ofemt_result`] object, including general information experimental grid, descriptive statistics, permutation-based test results.

title: Print method for `ofemt_result` objects — print.ofemt_result
code: # S3 method for class 'ofemt_result' print(x, ...)

title: Print method for `ofemt_result` objects — print.ofemt_result
text: x object class [`ofemt_result`], typically returned [ofemt()]. object must contain, minimum, `"General information"` data frame. Optionally, may include components `"Means comparison"` `"ANOVA permutation test"`. ... Additional arguments passed methods (ignored).

title: Print method for `ofemt_result` objects — print.ofemt_result
text: Invisibly returns input object `x`, unchanged.

title: Print method for `ofemt_result` objects — print.ofemt_result
text: print method provides human-readable summary console: **General information:** Cell size, number selected cells,     per-cell observation counts (minimum, median, maximum). **Spatial statistics:** Sample size (`n`), effective sample size     (`ESS`), spatial correlation (`Rho`), Moran’s **. **Means comparison:** Shows first 10 rows table     comparisons treatment means. **ANOVA permutation test:** Displays pairwise tests summarizing     median *p*-values corrected *p*-values across permutation runs. components missing, simply skipped printed output.

title: Print method for `ofemt_result` objects — print.ofemt_result
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   res <- ofemt(my_data, y = "Yield", x = "Treatment")   print(res) } # }

what: Filter grid by minimum observations and minimum number of treatments — select_grid
title: Filter grid by minimum observations and minimum number of treatments — select_grid
text: Spatially joins points grid, counts observations distinct treatments per cell, keeps cells satisfy thresholds.

title: Filter grid by minimum observations and minimum number of treatments — select_grid
code: select_grid(grid, data, x, min_per_cell = 1L, return_points = FALSE)

title: Filter grid by minimum observations and minimum number of treatments — select_grid
text: grid `ofe_grid` (`make_grid()`) `sf` polygons grid `CellID`. data `sf` object points. x Character scalar name treatment column `data`. min_per_cell Integer, minimum number observations per cell (default 1). return_points Logical, `TRUE` also returns joined points restricted selected cells (component `points_sel`). Default `FALSE`.

title: Filter grid by minimum observations and minimum number of treatments — select_grid
text: `ofe_grid` (list) updated `grid_sel` `params`. Adds: `cell_stats`: data.frame per-cell `n_obs`, `n_trt`, `treatments` list. `points_sel` (optional): points fall selected cells.

title: ofemeantest 0.0.900.9000
text: ofemt() exposes seed argument controls reproducibility permutation sampling. Default seed = 7L; pass NULL let results vary across runs. ofemt() accepts pre-built grid via grid argument. grid = NULL function builds one internally make_ofe_grid(); two paths interchangeable called matching arguments. make_ofe_grid() exported canonical way construct inspect analysis grid running test. plot_grid_selection() plot_pvalue_hist() added visual checks grid selection permutation p-value distribution. plot_pvalue_hist() base R fallback ggplot2 installed. print() method ofemt_result objects provides concise console summary. Two new vignettes: vignette("getting-started") (user manual) vignette("methodology") (technical description protocol). pkgdown website https://ppaccioretti.github.io/ofemeantest/. Dependencies reduced: dropped dplyr, magrittr tidyr Imports. ggplot2 moved Suggests. ofemt() rewritten top new grid pipeline; cells filtered select_grid(), twice. Moran’s now extracted name ($estimate["Moran statistic"]) instead position, dependency-contract test guarding future renames spdep. Test suite added (testthat 3rd edition) covering grid construction, end--end ofemt() ofe_f2, reproducibility seed, error paths, contracts upstream dependencies. Continuous integration GitHub Actions: R CMD check Ubuntu/macOS/Windows times R release+devel+oldrel; pkgdown site deployed push main. nmin_cell deprecated; use min_per_cell instead. alpha_bonferroni deprecated; use p_adjust_method = "bonferroni" instead.
